title: chionolaena lavandulifolia
intro: Chionolaena lavandulifolia, also known as Hawaiian lavender, is a flowering plant native to the Hawaiian Islands. It has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include relief from anxiety and insomnia, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. Chionolaena lavandulifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, making it a versatile and valuable medicinal plant in Hawaiian culture.
